ops.action: Converge validation/normalization into shared scope helpers and keep one canonical representation of scope strings to reduce branching bugs and doc drift.
ops.title: Centralize scope validation and normalization
ops.trigger: Scope logic starts spreading across multiple branches or call sites

ops.action: Update scope constants, parser/normalizer, and docs in the same change; add or adjust focused tests that cover accepted and rejected inputs so behavior cannot drift.
ops.title: Keep memory scope contracts synchronized
ops.trigger: Any change to memory scope kinds, parsing rules, or matching behavior

ops.action: Add explicit tests for positive matches, non-matches, malformed patterns, and normalization edge cases; keep failures user-facing and deterministic.
ops.title: Treat glob scope behavior as edge-case heavy
ops.trigger: Introducing or modifying glob-based memory scopes

ops.body:
# Loom Memory Scope Extension

Use this when adding or changing memory scope kinds in Loom.

## Procedure
1. Update the scope contract in `src/agent_loom/memory/constants.py` first (allowed kinds, exported sets, and any shared literals).
2. Implement parsing, validation, and canonicalization in `src/agent_loom/memory/scopes.py` with one canonical internal form for each scope.
3. Keep matching behavior deterministic and centralized; avoid duplicating scope interpretation logic in multiple call sites.
4. If glob behavior is involved, define clear handling for valid patterns, invalid patterns, and no-match cases with stable user-facing outcomes.
5. Update user-facing scope docs and examples in `src/agent_loom/memory/README.md` in the same change.
6. Add or update targeted tests (for example `tests/test_memory_scope_glob.py`) that cover:
   - successful parses and matches
   - explicit non-matches
   - malformed/invalid scope inputs
   - normalization/canonicalization behavior
7. Run quality gates before finalizing:
   - `uv run ruff check .`
   - `uv run basedpyright`
   - `uv run pytest` (or at minimum the relevant focused memory scope tests first, then full suite)

## Done Criteria
- Scope rules are defined in one contract source and consumed consistently.
- Parser/matcher behavior is deterministic and covered by focused tests.
- README scope docs match runtime behavior.
- No dead scope code paths remain.
ops.description: Procedure for adding or changing Loom memory scope kinds (including glob semantics) without introducing parser, matcher, or docs drift.
ops.name: loom-memory-scope-extension
